headline: Fiscal Responsibility Act promised in Budget 2024 — still not tabled
context: A Fiscal Responsibility Act announced during Budget 2024 has not been tabled in Parliament after two years, leaving Malaysia without a legal framework for fiscal discipline.

cards.big: Fiscal Responsibility Act promised 2 years ago — bill hasn't even been drafted
cards.sub: Government announced fiscal discipline law but shows no urgency to deliver it

cards.big: Federal Government Debt reached RM1.23 trillion or 65.8% of GDP in 2025
cards.sub: Debt ceiling is a political convention, not a legal requirement — it can be raised at will

cards.big: Debt servicing consumed RM52 billion in 2025 — 16% of total government revenue
cards.sub: Without fiscal rules, future generations bear the cost of today's borrowing

cards.big: New Zealand's Fiscal Responsibility Act 1994 became global model for fiscal governance
cards.sub: Three decades of international evidence shows fiscal legislation works

cards.big: Promising fiscal responsibility but never legislating it is the fiscal equivalent of a diet that starts Monday
Should Malaysia's fiscal rules have constitutional force to prevent political manipulation?
